title: FINS3666 2025 T1 Assessment 1 Activity 3 Student Guide V3
body:
Student Guide: Assessment 1, Activity 3

Proprietary Trading Strategies
Weight: 10%
Submission Deadline: 9:00pm Wednesday 9-Apr-2025.


1. Overview
This assessment involves designing a proprietary trading strategy using real world data from
the Australian Equities market. The trading strategy should incorporate data driven decision
making, especially with respect to risk and return.


2. Instructions for Students

2.1. Preparation:
   •   Review Key Concepts from Weeks 1-5, including: Statistical Analysis, Microstructure
       Metrics and Know Your Alpha
   •   Dataset Access: Download the historical order and trade data from Moodle.


2.2. In-Class Discussion: Proprietary Trading Strategies

Step 1: Data Processing, Microstructure Analysis
   •   Clean the dataset to handle missing values, outliers, etc if applicable
   •   Calculate standard microstructure metrics, as applicable.
Step 2: Proprietary Trading Strategy: Implementation
   •   Implement a simple proprietary trading strategy based on data from the Australian
       Equities market. The following steps are recommended:
          o   Trade Entry Criteria
          o   Trade Exit Criteria
                                                                                               CRICOS Provider Code 00098G




          o   Position Size Determination
          o   Trade Execution Rules.
     •    Bonus: Conduct basic performance and risk measurement of your proprietary trading
         strategy.


2.3. Post-Class Submission (Due Week 8)
Submit the following via Moodle:
    1. Analysis Report: Address the assessment questions concisely keeping to a maximum
       of 500 words excluding tables, charts or other tools that aid your report
    2. Code or Spreadsheet: Submit the code or spreadsheet used for your analysis
    3. Clean Dataset: If feasible, please provide the cleaned dataset used in your analysis.


3. Assessment Questions

3.1. Data Processing & Microstructure Analysis:
    •    What data processing steps did you perform and why were they important ?
    •    What microstructure analysis did you perform and why did you select those metrics ?
3.2. Proprietary Trading Strategy implementation:
    •    Summarise the results of the implementation of your proprietary trading strategy
    •    Explain any background analysis used to develop your trading strategy (this may
         overlap with your microstructure analysis).

3.3. Critical Reflection:
    •    Briefly discuss the strengths and limitations of your analysis
    •    How could your analysis be improved for real world practical application ? Provide
         specific ideas with reasoning, data requirements and expected outcomes.


4. Appendix: Background Analysis

Background market analysis is useful preparation required prior to implementation of your
proprietary trading strategy. Examples include:
    •    Trading Activity – Volumes, Average Trade Size, VWAP
    •    Order Book Activity –Bid Ask Spread, Bid & Ask Sizes, Order Imbalance
    •    Price Action – Moving Averages, Technical Indicators
    •    Risk Management – Volatility, Average True Range (ATR).
